label: Web
description: The browser-based frontend that users interact with to send requests and receive responses.

label: Mobile
description: The native iOS or Android app that connects to the backend over mobile networks.

label: DNS
description: Translates human-readable domain names into IP addresses so clients can reach your servers.

label: CDN
description: Caches static assets at edge locations worldwide to reduce latency for global users.

label: API Gateway
description: Routes all client requests to the correct backend service while enforcing auth and rate limits.

label: Load Balancer
description: Distributes incoming traffic across multiple servers to prevent any single point of failure.

label: Reverse Proxy
description: Sits in front of servers to handle SSL termination, caching, and request forwarding.

label: Server (Monolith)
description: A single deployable unit containing all application logic — simple to start, harder to scale.

label: Microservice
description: An independently deployable service responsible for a single business capability.

label: Serverless Function
description: Event-driven compute that scales to zero — you only pay when code is actually running.

label: Upload Service
description: Receives and validates file uploads, triggering the media processing pipeline without blocking the upload response.

label: User Service
description: Manages follower graph operations — follow, unfollow, block, and restrict — as a dedicated microservice separate from authentication.

label: Worker / Background Job
description: Processes tasks asynchronously in the background without blocking the main request flow.

label: Container (Docker / K8s)
description: Packages code and dependencies together for consistent, portable deployment across environments.

label: SQL Database
description: Stores structured relational data with ACID guarantees — ideal for transactions and complex queries.

label: NoSQL Database
description: Flexible schema database optimized for high-throughput reads and writes at massive scale.

label: Object Storage (S3-like)
description: Infinitely scalable storage for binary files like images, videos, and backups.

label: File System
description: Hierarchical file storage for structured data that needs direct filesystem access.

label: Search Engine
description: Full-text search index that enables fast, relevance-ranked queries across large datasets.

label: Data Warehouse
description: Columnar storage optimized for analytical queries across billions of historical records.

label: In-Memory Cache
description: Stores frequently accessed data in RAM for sub-millisecond read latency.

label: CDN Cache
description: Caches responses at edge nodes so repeat requests never reach the origin server.

label: Application Cache
description: In-process cache that stores computed results to avoid redundant work within a service.

label: Message Queue
description: Buffers messages between services so producers and consumers can operate at different speeds.

label: Event Bus / Pub-Sub
description: Broadcasts events to multiple subscribers so services react independently without tight coupling.

label: Kafka / Streaming
description: Processes millions of real-time events per second with durable, replayable log storage.

label: Webhook
description: Sends HTTP callbacks to external systems when specific events occur in your application.

label: Auth Service (JWT)
description: Validates user identity and manages access tokens to protect every secured endpoint.

label: OAuth / Identity Provider
description: Delegates authentication to a trusted third party like Google, GitHub, or Auth0.

label: API Key Manager
description: Issues and validates API keys to authenticate machine-to-machine service requests.

label: Firewall / WAF
description: Filters malicious traffic and blocks common web attacks before they reach your application.

label: Logger
description: Captures structured request traces and error details for debugging production issues.

label: Metrics Collector
description: Aggregates system health data like latency, error rate, and throughput in real time.

label: Tracing Service
description: Tracks a request's full journey across microservices to pinpoint latency bottlenecks.

label: Alert Manager
description: Fires notifications to on-call engineers when metrics breach defined thresholds.

label: Dashboard (Grafana)
description: Visualizes metrics and logs in real-time panels so teams can monitor system health at a glance.

label: LLM API (GPT / Claude)
description: Generates AI responses by sending conversation context to a large language model.

label: Vector Database
description: Stores embeddings for AI-powered similarity search and long-term memory retrieval.

label: Embedding Service
description: Converts text or images into dense vector representations for semantic search.

label: RAG Pipeline
description: Retrieves relevant context from a knowledge base and injects it into LLM prompts.

label: Model Server
description: Hosts and serves ML models as an API endpoint with batching and GPU optimization.

label: Email Service (SendGrid)
description: Sends transactional and marketing emails reliably at scale via a managed provider.

label: Payment Gateway (Stripe)
description: Handles payment processing, subscriptions, and fraud detection through a third-party provider.

label: SMS / Push Notification
description: Delivers real-time alerts to users via SMS or mobile push through APNs and FCM.

label: Maps API
description: Provides geolocation, routing, and map rendering capabilities from an external provider.

label: Generic 3rd Party API
description: Represents any external service your system integrates with via HTTP API.

label: CI/CD Pipeline
description: Automates building, testing, and deploying code changes on every commit.

label: Container Registry
description: Stores and versions Docker images so they can be pulled and deployed consistently.

label: Secret Manager
description: Securely stores and rotates API keys, passwords, and certificates used by services.

label: Config Service
description: Centralizes application configuration so settings can be changed without redeploying.

label: AI Agent
description: An autonomous AI that perceives its environment and takes actions to achieve a goal.

label: Agent Orchestrator
description: Coordinates multiple agents, routing tasks and aggregating their outputs.

label: Multi-Agent System
description: A network of specialized agents that collaborate to solve complex tasks.

label: Agent Memory
description: Persists an agent's past interactions and learned context across sessions.

label: Agent Planner
description: Breaks down high-level goals into ordered subtasks for the agent to execute.

label: Agent Executor
description: Runs the planned steps by calling tools and services on behalf of the agent.

label: Tool Registry
description: Catalogs available tools and APIs that agents can discover and invoke.

label: Agent Supervisor
description: Monitors agent behavior and intervenes when outputs violate safety constraints.

label: Reflection Module
description: Lets an agent critique and revise its own outputs before returning a final answer.

label: Task Decomposer
description: Splits a complex task into smaller, parallelizable subtasks for efficient execution.

label: Agent Router
description: Directs incoming requests to the most appropriate specialized agent.

label: Critic Agent
description: Evaluates another agent's output and provides structured feedback for improvement.

label: OpenAI GPT-4
description: OpenAI's flagship model for complex reasoning, coding, and instruction following.

label: Anthropic Claude
description: Anthropic's safety-focused LLM with a large context window and strong reasoning.

label: Google Gemini
description: Google's multimodal LLM capable of processing text, images, audio, and video.

label: Mistral
description: Efficient open-weight LLM optimized for speed and performance on limited hardware.

label: Meta LLaMA
description: Meta's open-source LLM family that can be self-hosted for full data privacy.

label: Local LLM (Ollama)
description: Runs open-source LLMs locally on your own hardware with no external API calls.

label: LLM Gateway
description: Unified API layer that routes requests across multiple LLM providers with fallback.

label: LLM Router
description: Selects the best LLM for each request based on cost, latency, or capability.

label: LLM Load Balancer
description: Distributes LLM requests across multiple model instances to maximize throughput.

label: Model Registry
description: Catalogs versioned model artifacts with metadata for reproducible deployments.

label: Fine-tuned Model
description: A base LLM further trained on domain-specific data to improve task performance.

label: Multimodal Model
description: Processes and generates across multiple modalities like text, images, and audio.

label: RAG Pipeline
description: Retrieves relevant documents and injects them as context into LLM prompts.

label: Document Loader
description: Ingests documents from various sources like PDFs, URLs, and databases into the pipeline.

label: Text Splitter / Chunker
description: Divides long documents into smaller chunks that fit within the model's context window.

label: Embedding Model
description: Converts text chunks into dense vectors that capture semantic meaning for similarity search.

label: Vector Store
description: Persists and indexes vector embeddings for fast approximate nearest-neighbor retrieval.

label: Retriever
description: Queries the vector store to find the most semantically relevant chunks for a given input.

label: Reranker
description: Re-scores retrieved chunks by relevance to improve the quality of context passed to the LLM.

label: Context Builder
description: Assembles retrieved chunks into a coherent context block for the LLM prompt.

label: Query Expander
description: Generates multiple query variants to improve recall from the vector store.

label: Hybrid Search
description: Combines keyword and semantic search to maximize retrieval precision and recall.

label: Knowledge Graph
description: Stores entities and relationships as a graph for structured knowledge retrieval.

label: Document Parser
description: Extracts clean text and structure from raw documents like PDFs and HTML pages.

label: Semantic Cache
description: Caches LLM responses for semantically similar queries to reduce cost and latency.

label: Pinecone
description: Managed vector database optimized for low-latency similarity search at scale.

label: Weaviate
description: Open-source vector database with built-in ML model integrations and GraphQL API.

label: Qdrant
description: High-performance vector search engine with rich filtering and payload support.

label: ChromaDB
description: Lightweight open-source embedding database designed for AI application prototyping.

label: Milvus
description: Cloud-native vector database built for billion-scale similarity search workloads.

label: pgvector (Postgres)
description: Adds vector similarity search directly to PostgreSQL without a separate database.

label: FAISS (Local)
description: Facebook's in-memory library for fast approximate nearest-neighbor search on CPU/GPU.

label: Training Pipeline
description: Orchestrates the end-to-end workflow of preparing data, training, and evaluating a model.

label: Data Preprocessing
description: Cleans, normalizes, and transforms raw data into a format suitable for model training.

label: Feature Store
description: Centralizes computed features so they can be reused consistently across training and serving.

label: Model Trainer
description: Executes the training loop, updating model weights using the prepared dataset.

label: Hyperparameter Tuner
description: Searches for the optimal model configuration through automated experimentation.

label: Experiment Tracker
description: Records metrics, parameters, and artifacts for every training run to enable comparison.

label: Model Evaluator
description: Measures model performance on held-out data using task-specific metrics.

label: Dataset Store
description: Stores versioned training datasets for reproducible model training runs.

label: Data Versioning (DVC)
description: Tracks changes to datasets and model files alongside code in version control.

label: GPU Cluster
description: Pool of GPU-accelerated machines for distributed model training at scale.

label: Distributed Training
description: Splits training across multiple GPUs or machines to handle large models and datasets.

label: Fine-tuning Pipeline
description: Adapts a pre-trained model to a specific domain using a smaller labeled dataset.

label: Model Server
description: Exposes trained ML models as REST or gRPC endpoints for real-time inference.

label: Inference Engine
description: Optimized runtime that executes model predictions with minimal latency.

label: Triton Inference Server
description: NVIDIA's high-performance inference server supporting multiple ML frameworks.

label: TorchServe
description: PyTorch's official model serving framework for deploying models in production.

label: Model Cache
description: Keeps loaded model weights in memory to eliminate cold-start latency on requests.

label: Batch Inference
description: Processes large volumes of predictions offline in batches for cost efficiency.

label: Streaming Inference
description: Runs model predictions on continuous data streams in near real time.

label: Model Quantizer
description: Reduces model precision to lower memory usage and speed up inference.

label: A/B Testing (ML)
description: Routes traffic between model versions to compare performance on live users.

label: Shadow Deployment
description: Runs a new model in parallel with production to validate it without user impact.

label: MLflow
description: Open-source platform for tracking experiments, packaging models, and managing deployments.

label: Weights & Biases
description: Experiment tracking and visualization platform for monitoring ML training runs.

label: Model Monitor
description: Continuously checks live model predictions for quality degradation over time.

label: Data Drift Detector
description: Detects when incoming data distribution shifts away from the training distribution.

label: Model Drift Detector
description: Alerts when model accuracy degrades due to changes in real-world patterns.

label: Prediction Logger
description: Records every model input and output for auditing, debugging, and retraining.

label: Feedback Collector
description: Gathers user feedback on model outputs to build labeled datasets for retraining.

label: Retraining Trigger
description: Automatically kicks off a new training run when drift or performance thresholds are breached.

label: Model Versioning
description: Tracks model versions with metadata so any previous version can be rolled back.

label: Explainability Service
description: Generates human-readable explanations for why a model made a specific prediction.

label: Prompt Template
description: Defines reusable prompt structures with variables for consistent LLM interactions.

label: Prompt Registry
description: Stores and versions prompt templates so teams can manage and iterate on them.

label: Prompt Optimizer
description: Automatically improves prompt wording to maximize model output quality.

label: Output Parser
description: Extracts structured data from raw LLM text responses reliably.

label: Structured Output
description: Forces the LLM to return responses in a defined JSON or typed schema.

label: Guardrails / Safety
description: Validates and filters LLM outputs to prevent harmful or off-policy responses.

label: Content Filter
description: Screens inputs and outputs for prohibited content before they reach users.

label: Hallucination Detector
description: Identifies factually incorrect or unsupported claims in LLM-generated text.

label: Token Counter
description: Measures token usage per request to manage costs and stay within context limits.

label: Context Window Manager
description: Trims or summarizes conversation history to keep prompts within the model's token limit.

label: LLM Response Cache
description: Caches identical LLM requests to avoid redundant API calls and reduce cost.

label: Cost Tracker
description: Monitors and attributes LLM API spending across teams, features, and users.

label: LangChain
description: Framework for building LLM-powered apps with chains, agents, and memory.

label: LangGraph
description: Builds stateful multi-agent workflows as directed graphs with LangChain.

label: LlamaIndex
description: Data framework for connecting LLMs to external knowledge sources via RAG.

label: AutoGen
description: Microsoft's framework for building conversational multi-agent AI systems.

label: CrewAI
description: Orchestrates role-based AI agent crews that collaborate on complex tasks.

label: DSPy
description: Programmatic framework for optimizing LLM pipelines through automatic prompt tuning.

label: Haystack
description: Open-source NLP framework for building search and question-answering pipelines.

label: Semantic Kernel
description: Microsoft's SDK for integrating LLMs into applications with plugins and planners.

label: Instructor
description: Enforces structured Pydantic outputs from LLM calls with automatic validation and retry.

label: Data Ingestion
description: Pulls raw data from various sources into the pipeline for processing.

label: ETL Pipeline
description: Extracts, transforms, and loads data between systems in a structured workflow.

label: Data Labeling Service
description: Annotates raw data with ground-truth labels for supervised model training.

label: Data Validation
description: Checks data quality and schema conformance before it enters the training pipeline.

label: Synthetic Data Generator
description: Creates artificial training data to augment real datasets or protect privacy.

label: Data Catalog
description: Indexes and documents available datasets so teams can discover and reuse them.

label: Web Scraper
description: Automatically extracts structured data from web pages for training or enrichment.

label: PDF Extractor
description: Parses text, tables, and metadata from PDF documents for downstream processing.

label: OCR Service
description: Converts scanned images and documents into machine-readable text.

label: Speech-to-Text (STT)
description: Transcribes spoken audio into text for voice-driven applications.

label: Text-to-Speech (TTS)
description: Converts written text into natural-sounding spoken audio.

label: Whisper (OpenAI)
description: OpenAI's open-source speech recognition model with multilingual transcription support.

label: ElevenLabs TTS
description: High-quality AI voice synthesis with cloning and emotional tone control.

label: Audio Processor
description: Applies noise reduction, normalization, and feature extraction to audio signals.

label: Voice Activity Detector
description: Identifies segments of audio that contain speech versus silence or noise.

label: Image Classifier
description: Assigns category labels to images using a trained convolutional or transformer model.

label: Object Detector
description: Locates and identifies multiple objects within an image with bounding boxes.

label: Image Embedder
description: Converts images into dense vector representations for similarity search.

label: Image Generator
description: Creates new images from text prompts using diffusion or GAN models.

label: Vision LLM (GPT-4V)
description: Understands and reasons about image content alongside text in a single model.

label: AI OCR
description: Uses deep learning to extract text from complex layouts, handwriting, and low-quality scans.

label: Video Analyzer
description: Processes video frames to detect events, classify content, or extract metadata.

label: Diffusion Model
description: Generates high-quality images by iteratively denoising random noise guided by a prompt.

label: WebRTC Server
description: Handles peer-to-peer audio and video connections using the WebRTC protocol.

label: TURN Server
description: Relays media traffic when direct peer-to-peer connection fails due to NAT or firewall.

label: STUN Server
description: Discovers the public IP address of a client behind NAT for WebRTC connection setup.

label: Media Server
description: Processes, mixes, and distributes audio and video streams in real-time.

label: Signaling Server
description: Coordinates WebRTC connection setup by exchanging session descriptions between peers.

label: Graph Database
description: Stores and queries relationships between entities using graph structures optimized for social connections.

label: Feed Ranker
description: ML model that scores and ranks content for personalized feed generation.

label: Fan-out Service
description: Distributes a single event to many recipients — used in social feed delivery at scale.

label: Timeline Service
description: Assembles and serves personalized user timelines by merging followed accounts' content.

label: Trending Service
description: Computes trending topics in real-time using sliding window counters across geographic regions.

label: CRDT Engine
description: Conflict-free Replicated Data Type engine that merges concurrent edits without conflicts.

label: Presence Service
description: Tracks which users are online, their cursor positions, and active document sections.

label: Canvas Renderer
description: Renders vector graphics and design elements on an infinite canvas with GPU acceleration.

label: Version History
description: Stores immutable snapshots of document state for time-travel and version restoration.

label: Inventory Service
description: Manages real-time stock levels with pessimistic locking to prevent overselling.

label: Cart Service
description: Manages user shopping cart state with session persistence and price recalculation.

label: Checkout Service
description: Orchestrates the checkout flow including cart validation, payment, and order creation.

label: Order Service
description: Manages order lifecycle from creation through fulfillment, returns, and refunds.

label: Tax Service
description: Calculates applicable taxes based on buyer location, product type, and merchant nexus.

label: Fulfillment Service
description: Routes orders to warehouses and third-party logistics providers for physical delivery.

label: Dasher Service
description: Manages delivery driver state, availability zones, and assignment optimization.

label: ETA Service
description: Predicts delivery arrival times using ML models trained on historical delivery data.

label: Routing Engine
description: Calculates optimal delivery routes considering traffic, distance, and multiple stops.

label: Geofence Service
description: Detects when drivers enter or exit defined geographic boundaries to trigger events.

label: Git Object Storage
description: Content-addressable storage for Git objects — blobs, trees, commits, and tags.

label: CI Runner
description: Executes build, test, and lint jobs in isolated containers for continuous integration.

label: Code Review Service
description: Manages pull request workflows including diff rendering, review assignments, and merge checks.

label: Webhook Dispatcher
description: Delivers HTTP webhook events to external services with retry logic and delivery guarantees.

label: Package Registry
description: Stores and serves versioned software packages — npm, Maven, PyPI, Docker images.

label: Audio Transcoder
description: Converts audio files between formats and bitrates for different device capabilities.

label: Audio CDN
description: Delivers audio streams from edge locations close to listeners for low-latency playback.

label: Offline Sync Service
description: Syncs locally cached content with server state when device reconnects to network.

label: Playlist Service
description: Manages user playlists, collaborative playlists, and algorithmic playlist generation.

label: Trust & Safety Service
description: Detects and prevents fraud, abuse, and policy violations using ML and rule engines.

label: Review Service
description: Manages user reviews and ratings with fraud detection and sentiment analysis.

label: Pricing Engine
description: Calculates dynamic prices based on demand, supply, time, and market conditions.

label: Availability Service
description: Manages listing calendars, booking windows, and real-time availability for rentals.

label: Circuit Breaker
description: Prevents cascading failures by stopping requests to a failing service until it recovers.

label: Notification Service
description: Manages delivery of in-app, push, email, and SMS notifications triggered by system events.

label: ML Service
description: Serves trained machine learning model predictions in real-time via a REST or gRPC API.

label: Recommendation Service
description: Generates personalized content recommendations using collaborative filtering and ML models.

label: Feed Service
description: Assembles and serves the personalized home feed by merging content from followed accounts.

label: Data Ingestion Service
description: Receives, validates, and normalizes incoming event streams from external sources and SDKs.

label: Driver App
description: Native mobile app used by Uber drivers to receive trip requests, navigate, and manage earnings.

label: Location Service
description: Ingests and processes high-frequency GPS location updates from driver devices in real time.

label: Fraud Detection Service
description: Detects payment fraud, fake GPS routes, and account abuse using ML signals per transaction.

label: Sidecar Proxy (Envoy)
description: Handles mTLS, retries, circuit breaking, and observability at the pod level — transparently intercepting all service-to-service traffic.

label: Service Mesh (Istio)
description: Manages east-west traffic between services with automatic mTLS, load balancing, and policy enforcement across the entire cluster.

label: Control Plane
description: Configures the service mesh policies, routes, and security rules — the brain that programs all sidecar proxies.

label: Token Bucket Rate Limiter
description: Enforces rate limits using the token bucket algorithm — allowing burst traffic up to a bucket size while maintaining a steady average rate.

label: Sliding Window Rate Limiter
description: Enforces rate limits across a sliding time window — more accurate than fixed windows with no burst at window boundaries.

label: Leaky Bucket Rate Limiter
description: Smooths out traffic bursts by processing requests at a constant rate regardless of input burst intensity.

label: Distributed Rate Limiter
description: Uses Redis to coordinate rate limiting across multiple server instances — ensuring accurate limits even with horizontal scaling.

label: CDC Connector (Debezium)
description: Captures row-level changes from database transaction logs — enabling streaming CDC to Kafka without database query overhead.

label: Event Store (EventStoreDB)
description: Stores events as an immutable, append-only log — enabling event sourcing where the entire system state is reconstructed by replaying events.

label: CQRS Command Handler
description: Handles write operations — validating and persisting commands to the write model with strict consistency guarantees.

label: CQRS Query Handler
description: Serves read operations from a denormalized read model — optimized for fast queries without the overhead of translating from a normalized write model.

label: Saga Orchestrator
description: Coordinates multi-service distributed transactions using compensating actions — instead of ACID locks, each step has a defined rollback.

label: Saga Participant
description: Executes a single step in a distributed transaction and implements compensating logic for rollback when other steps fail.

label: Raft Consensus (etcd)
description: Maintains consistent state across a distributed cluster using the Raft consensus algorithm — leader election, log replication, and membership changes.

label: Distributed Lock (Redis)
description: Coordinates access to shared resources across multiple servers — preventing race conditions when multiple instances attempt concurrent writes.

label: Leader Election
description: Elects a single leader from a cluster of nodes — the leader handles all writes while followers replicate for fault tolerance.

label: Service Discovery (Consul)
description: Tracks live service instances and their network locations — enabling services to find each other without hardcoded IP addresses.

label: Config Sync (etcd)
description: Distributes configuration changes across all service instances in real time — ensuring consistent config without restarts.

label: BFF Gateway
description: Backend-for-Frontend pattern — a dedicated API layer per client type (web, mobile, TV) aggregating data shaped specifically for that interface.

label: GraphQL Federation Gateway
description: Combines multiple GraphQL schemas from different services into a unified supergraph — clients query one endpoint while services own their subgraph.

label: GraphQL Subgraph
description: Owns a portion of the unified GraphQL schema — responsible for resolving types and fields within its domain.

label: API Composition Layer
description: Aggregates data from multiple backend services into a single API response — flattening microservice boundaries for specific use cases.

label: mTLS Certificate Authority
description: Issues and rotates TLS certificates for service-to-service authentication — enabling mutual TLS where both client and server verify each other.

label: OAuth 2.0 + PKCE Flow
description: Secure authorization flow for mobile apps — PKCE prevents authorization code interception by requiring a dynamically generated code verifier.

label: JWT Validation Service
description: Validates and parses JWTs at the API gateway — verifying signature, expiration, and claims without hitting the auth server on every request.

label: Token Rotation Service
description: Automatically rotates short-lived tokens without user interaction — refresh tokens are rotated on every use with stolen token detection.

label: Zero Trust Proxy
description: Enforces authentication and authorization on every request regardless of network location — no implicit trust based on network perimeter.

label: OpenTelemetry Collector
description: Centralized collector for traces, metrics, and logs — processing and exporting telemetry data to multiple backends (Jaeger, Prometheus, Elasticsearch).

label: Correlation ID Injector
description: Injects a unique correlation ID into every request that propagates across all service calls — enabling end-to-end request tracing.

label: SLO/SLI Tracker
description: Monitors Service Level Objectives against Service Level Indicators — tracking availability targets and alerting when error budgets burn.

label: Error Budget Monitor
description: Tracks the remaining error budget for an SLO — alerting when the budget burns faster than acceptable and prompting reliability work.

label: Structured Logger
description: Emits logs in JSON format with consistent field schemas — enabling log aggregation and querying across all services.

label: Read Replica
description: An asynchronously replicated copy of the primary database optimized for read-heavy workloads — reducing load on the writer by offloading queries.

label: Database Shard
description: A horizontal partition of the database containing a subset of rows — distributing write load across multiple database instances.

label: Connection Pooler (PgBouncer)
description: Multiplexes hundreds of application connections over a small pool of database connections — preventing connection exhaustion at high concurrency.

label: Change Data Cache
description: Caches the results of frequent queries invalidated by database writes — using CDC events to know exactly when to invalidate stale entries.

label: Cache Stampede Prevention
description: Prevents thundering herd when cache expires — using probabilistic early expiration or locking to ensure only one request rebuilds the cache.

label: Write-Through Cache
description: Updates both cache and database synchronously on every write — guaranteeing cache consistency but adding latency to write operations.

label: Cache-Aside Pattern
description: Application manages cache explicitly — checking cache first on read, populating on miss, and updating cache on write alongside the database.

label: Anycast DNS Router
description: Routes users to the nearest PoP using BGP anycast — the same IP announces from multiple locations and users reach the geographically closest one.

label: DDoS Mitigation Layer
description: Scrubs malicious traffic at the network edge before it reaches origin — absorbing volumetric attacks using anycast distribution and rate limiting.

label: Prefetch Cache
description: Proactively caches content before users request it based on prediction algorithms — reducing latency to near zero for anticipated requests.

label: Consistent Hashing Ring
description: Maps data to nodes using a hash ring — minimizing data movement when nodes join or leave compared to traditional modulo-based hashing.

label: Virtual Nodes
description: Adds multiple virtual positions per physical node on the consistent hash ring — improving load distribution balance across nodes.

label: Tiered CDN Cache
description: Organizes CDN caches in tiers — L1 (edge PoP), L2 (regional), L3 (origin shield) — minimizing origin requests while maximizing cache hit rates.

label: Surrogate Key Purger
description: Invalidates CDN cache entries using soft purge with surrogate keys — enabling instant purging of related content without tracking individual URLs.
